2604.16505.title: Predicting Blastocyst Formation in IVF: Integrating DINOv2 and Attention-Based LSTM on Time-Lapse Embryo Images
2604.16505.paper_summary: The selection of the optimal embryo for transfer is a critical yet challenging step in in vitro fertilization (IVF), primarily due to its reliance on the manual inspection of extensive time-lapse imaging data. A key obstacle in this process is predicting blastocyst formation from the limited number of daily images available. Many clinics also lack complete time-lapse systems, so full videos are often unavailable. In this study, we aimed to predict which embryos will develop into blastocysts using limited daily images from time-lapse recordings. We propose a novel hybrid model that combines DINOv2, a transformer-based vision model, with an enhanced long short-term memory (LSTM) network featuring a multi-head attention layer. DINOv2 extracts meaningful features from embryo images, and the LSTM model then uses these features to analyze embryo development over time and generate final predictions. We tested our model on a real dataset of 704 embryo videos. The model achieved 96.4% accuracy, surpassing existing methods. It also performs well with missing frames, making it valuable for many IVF laboratories with limited imaging systems. Our approach can assist embryologists in selecting better embryos more efficiently and with greater confidence.
2604.16505.paper_summary_zh: 選擇最佳胚胎進行移植是體外受精（IVF）中一個關鍵但具挑戰性的步驟，主要因為這一過程依賴於對大量時間延遲影像數據的手動檢查。這一過程中的一個主要障礙是從每日有限的影像中預測囊胚的形成。許多診所也缺乏完整的時間延遲系統，因此完整的影片往往無法獲得。在本研究中，我們的目標是利用來自時間延遲錄影的每日有限影像預測哪些胚胎會發展成囊胚。我們提出了一種新穎的混合模型，將基於Transformer的視覺模型DINOv2與增強的長短期記憶（LSTM）網絡相結合，後者具有多頭注意力層。DINOv2從胚胎影像中提取有意義的特徵，然後LSTM模型利用這些特徵分析胚胎隨時間的發展並生成最終預測。我們在704個胚胎影片的真實數據集上測試了我們的模型。該模型達到了96.4%的準確率，超越了現有的方法。它在缺失幀的情況下也表現良好，對於許多擁有有限影像系統的IVF實驗室來說非常有價值。我們的方法可以幫助胚胎學家更有效且更有信心地選擇更好的胚胎。
2604.16505.author: Zahra Asghari Varzaneh et.al.
2604.16505.authors: Zahra Asghari Varzaneh, Niclas Wölner-Hanssen, Reza Khoshkangini, Thomas Ebner, Magnus Johnsson
